grades.expectation: Response notes the beneficiary's own extension as relevant to enforceability (acquiescence/waiver argument)
grades.reason: Clearly identifies the 2015 extension as creating acquiescence and undermining enforceability

grades.expectation: Response discusses whether indemnity insurance is available and the 'no approach' prerequisite
grades.reason: Mentions indemnity insurance as an option but doesn't discuss the 'no approach' prerequisite or availability conditions

grades.expectation: Response mentions s84 LPA 1925 ground (aa) — covenant impedes reasonable use of land
grades.reason: References s84 LPA 1925 but only mentions obsolete/no practical benefit grounds, not ground (aa) reasonable use

grades.expectation: Response distinguishes between a front/visible extension and rear extension in terms of risk
grades.reason: No distinction made between front/visible and rear extensions in risk assessment

grades.expectation: Response provides practical advice on sequencing: decide insurance vs release before any contact
grades.reason: No advice given on sequencing decisions or avoiding contact with beneficiary before deciding approach
